canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, the name 'AIR-Bench' refers to several distinct benchmarks, leading to considerable variation in its definition and use. The most prevalent framing describes it as an aggregated evaluation suite for Large Audio Models, designed to measure audio understanding and general auditory processing across speech, music, and audio tasks. Some papers in this vein note that it extends to open-ended question-answering formats, while one specifies that its conversational evaluation component, AirBench-Chat, relies on text-based interactions. A separate line of work defines AIR-Bench as a benchmark for measuring information retrieval performance, particularly on out-of-domain topics, with one paper noting its test data is "automatically generated by large language models." A third, distinct characterization appears in a single paper, which presents 'AIR-BENCH 2024' as a language-model safety benchmark containing thousands of "risky instruction prompts directly grounded in the current regulatory landscape." Thus, depending on the paper, AIR-Bench is used to evaluate audio processing, information retrieval, or model safety.

definitions.text: An aggregated evaluation suite for Large Audio Models, covering a range of audio understanding tasks.
definitions.paper: COSMMIC: Comment-Sensitive Multimodal MultilingualIndian Corpus for Summarization and Headline Generation

definitions.text: A benchmark for audio comprehension that uses text-based interactions for its conversational ability evaluation (AirBench-Chat).
definitions.paper: VoxDialogue: Can Spoken Dialogue Systems Understand Information Beyond Words?

definitions.text: A benchmark that includes tasks from speech, music, and audio, with a specific track for evaluating foundation models.
definitions.paper: Dynamic-SUPERB Phase-2: A Collaboratively Expanding Benchmark for Measuring the Capabilities of Spoken Language Models with 180 Tasks

definitions.text: An information retrieval benchmark that covers a range of out-of-domain topics, including long document and question-answering sections, to test model generalization.
definitions.paper: NV-Embed: Improved Techniques for Training LLMs as Generalist Embedding Models

definitions.text: A language-model safety benchmark containing 5,694 risky instruction prompts grounded in the AIR 2024 taxonomy of regulation- and policy-derived risk categories.
definitions.paper: AIR-BENCH 2024: A Safety Benchmark based on Regulation and Policies Specified Risk Categories
